title: Login field content pasted from clipboard bypasses format validation and is correctly rejected when pasted value is malformed
description: Validates that paste events do not silently bypass field-level format validation. A widespread production bug class: 'oninput' or 'onkeyup' validation handlers are skipped for programmatic paste (ctrl+v / right-click paste), so a malformed email address with no '@' or extra spaces pasted into the field passes client-side checks and reaches the server. The server either throws 500 or returns an ambiguous error that doesn't help the user.
expected_result: PASS: Pasting a malformed email triggers format validation on blur AND blocks submission; the error appears before the API is called; URL stays at /login; no server error response is generated. FAIL: The malformed pasted email bypasses format validation, a network request fires to the auth API with the invalid email, the server returns a 400/422/500 shown as a raw error or silent failure, or the leading space is silently trimmed server-side and the value matches a real account — authenticating an unintended user.

test_steps.expected: Login page loads with email/username and password input fields visible

test_steps.action: Click on the email/username field to focus it
Paste a malformed email string (no domain, leading space) into the email field using keyboard shortcut
test_steps.expected: Malformed email ' marcus.thibodeau@' (leading space, no domain) is pasted into the field

test_steps.action: Press Tab to move focus away from the email field, triggering blur/change validation
test_steps.expected: Focus moves to the next field; inline email format validation fires on blur

test_steps.action: Verify that an email format validation error appears after blur
test_steps.expected: Inline error message like 'Please enter a valid email address' is visible beneath the email field

test_steps.action: Fill in the password field with a non-empty password to avoid a second validation error masking the email error
Click the submit button to attempt form submission with the pasted malformed email
test_steps.expected: Form submission is blocked; the email validation error is still displayed; no API call is fired

test_steps.action: Verify email error message remains visible after attempted submission
test_steps.expected: Email field still shows format validation error; form has not been submitted

test_steps.action: Verify the page URL has not changed (user was not redirected or shown a server error)
test_steps.expected: URL remains /login; no server 400/422 error page is displayed
